title: phragmites australis subsp. maximus
intro: Phragmites australis subsp. maximus, also known as the giant reed or common reed, is a perennial, aquatic plant native to Europe, Asia, and Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The health benefits of Phragmites australis subsp. maximus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support respiratory health, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's rhizomes and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
